chapter_title: Logical Alphabet and Number Sequence Test

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If the first and the second digits of each number are interchanged, which number will be the second highest?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If 382 is written as 238, 473 as 347 and so on, then which of the following two numbers will have least difference between them?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If the first and the third digits of each number are interchanged and one is added to the second digit of each number, then which of the following pairs of numbers will have highest total of their numerical value?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If the first digit of each number replaces the third digit of that number, third digit replaces the second digit and the second digit replaces the first digit and then the numbers thus formed are arranged in the descending order, then which number will be the third?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If one is added to the second digit of each number and one is subtracted from the third digit of each number and then if the first and third digits of each number are interchanged, then which number will be lowest?

solutions.solution:
(D) Let us see
The given number 5 3 2 6 9 7 1 8
Ascending order arrangement 1 2 3 5 6 7 8 9
So, position of only one digit remains unchanged.

(C) Let us see
The given number 5 9 1 6 4 8 2 3
Descending order arrangement 9 8 6 5 4 3 2 1
So, position of two digits remain unchanged.

(C) Let us see
The given number 6 2 5 9 1 4 8 3
Ascending order arrangement 1 2 3 4 5 6 8 9
Hence, two digits remain at the same place from the beginning.

(D) Let us see
The given number 5 2 3 4 8 1 6
Descending order arrangement 8 6 5 4 3 2 1
∴ Required pairs = (53), (23), (34) and (24)

(C) Let us see
The given number 5 3 6 1 4 2
Ascending order arrangement 1 2 3 4 5 6
So, there are two required pair of digits.

(C) Let us see
The given number 8 2 4 7 5 3 1
Ascending order arrangement 1 2 3 4 5 7 8
So, position of two digits remain unchanged.

(d) Original number 8 6 4 3 5 1 9 2
Ascending order 1 2 3 4 5 6 8 9
From left 4th, From 2nd right
∴ Required difference = 8 - 4 = 4

(C) Let us see
Original number 9 2 5 8 1 4 7 3
Ascending order 1 2 3 4 5 7 8 9
From left 3rd, From right 4th
∴ Required sum = 5 + 3 = 8
